title: de Sitter Vacua & pUniverses
authors: Jeremias Aguilera-Damia, Dionysios Anninos, Tarek Anous, Johnny Gleeson, Alan Rios Fukelman
abstract: We analyze a simple extension of the Schwinger model, which we refer to as the $p$-Schwinger model, on a de Sitter background. In this theory, the charged massless fermions carry non-unit integer charge $p$. In Minkowski space, the $p$-Schwinger model has discrete zero- and one-form global symmetries that are spontaneously broken, yielding $p$ degenerate ground states. We demonstrate that these features persist upon placing the $p$-Schwinger model on a global de Sitter background, establishing that such discrete global symmetries can be spontaneously broken for quantum field theories in de Sitter space. In particular, the theory is endowed with $p$ distinct, but locally-indistinguishable, de Sitter invariant states, the de Sitter vacua, satisfying the Hadamard property. We couple a variant of the $p$-Schwinger model with ${\rm N}_{\rm f}$ flavors to quantum gravity with $Λ>0$, and demonstrate the existence of a semiclassical de Sitter saddle at large ${\rm N}_{\rm f}$. In the gravitational theory, the $p$ de Sitter invariant vacua are speculatively interpreted as microstates of the de Sitter horizon in the low-energy effective field theory.
summary: This paper explores the persistence of discrete global symmetries in de Sitter space using the p-Schwinger model. It shows that spontaneous symmetry breaking leads to multiple degenerate de Sitter vacua, which can be interpreted as microstates of the cosmological horizon. The work provides a formal path-integral construction for these states in a gravitational context.
main_problem: Investigating whether discrete global symmetries (zero-form and one-form) and their spontaneous breaking patterns persist when moving from Minkowski space to a de Sitter background.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that the Zp symmetry breaking pattern persists in de Sitter space, leading to p distinct, locally indistinguishable de Sitter vacua which may be interpreted as microstates of the de Sitter horizon.
method: The study utilizes bosonization, the Hartle-Hawking Euclidean path integral construction, and a large Nf semiclassical analysis in 2D quantum gravity.
model_or_system: The p-Schwinger model (a 2D QED extension with fermions of non-unit integer charge p) and an Abelian BF theory, both analyzed on a global de Sitter background and coupled to 2D quantum gravity.
key_observables: Electric field two-point function, Wilson line expectation value, chiral condensate, meson n-point functions, and topological line/point operators.
important_parameters: Integer charge p, number of fermion flavors Nf, cosmological constant Lambda, and the de Sitter length scale.
main_assumptions: The identification of vacua as horizon microstates is speculative; the analysis of symmetry breaking via wormholes is heuristic; and the results may be specific to 2D geometries.
paper_structure: The paper begins by analyzing the symmetry structure of the p-Schwinger model and BF theory, moves to a path-integral construction of states on a sphere, extends the model to include dynamical 2D gravity in the large Nf limit, and concludes with a discussion on the stability of these symmetries against gravitational effects like wormholes.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: Screening of the electric field created by an j = 1 Wilson line in the p = 1-Schwinger model.
